wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SonicStream project overview section. Explain the purpose of this modern music streaming platform, its core value proposition, and target audience. Document the key features including music discovery, audio player, social features, and user management. Describe the technology stack combination of Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, and Prisma ORM. Include architectural highlights such as component-based design, centralized state management with Zustand, and real-time data fetching with React Query. Provide context about the project's scope, goals, and positioning in the music streaming ecosystem. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both beginners and experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for SonicStream's React-based interface. Detail the Next.js App Router implementation with file-based routing, dynamic routes for content discovery, and page component organization. Document the component hierarchy starting from RootLayout through Sidebar navigation, Player controls, and reusable card components. Explain the provider pattern implementation for global state management including QueryProvider for React Query and ThemeProvider for styling. Cover component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and state management integration. Include responsive design architecture, animation systems using Motion, and accessibility considerations. Address component lifecycle, performance optimization through code splitting, and integration with external APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for SonicStream's authentication endpoints. Document the main authentication route (/api/auth) with POST method supporting signup and signin actions, including request/response schemas, password hashing implementation, and avatar upload functionality. Cover the forgot password endpoint (/api/auth/forgot) for password reset initiation and the reset password endpoint (/api/auth/reset) for completing password recovery. Include detailed parameter descriptions, authentication flow diagrams, session management, error handling strategies, and security considerations. Provide client implementation examples, common use cases, and integration guidelines for frontend applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Music Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the music discovery feature. Cover the trending content carousel, mood-based recommendations, popular artists showcase, and featured playlists functionality. Document the implementation of infinite scrolling for artist sections, content normalization from external APIs, and performance optimization strategies. Explain how the discovery system integrates with the external music API, handles content loading and caching, and manages user engagement metrics. Include examples of content categorization, recommendation algorithms, and user interaction patterns. Address data fetching strategies, lazy loading implementations, and responsive design considerations for different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: User and Authentication Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for SonicStream's User and authentication-related models. Detail the User model with role-based access control (USER/ADMIN), email uniqueness constraints, password hash storage, and avatar management. Document the PasswordReset model for secure password recovery including token generation, expiration handling, and cascade deletion policies. Explain the Role enum implementation and its impact on authorization flows. Include field definitions, data types, constraints, and business rules. Document the relationship patterns showing how users connect to their liked songs, playlists, queue items, followed artists, and password reset tokens. Address data validation requirements, security considerations for password storage, and access control mechanisms. Provide examples of common queries and operations performed on these models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SonicStream's core UI components that form the foundation of the music streaming interface. Document the Player component's advanced audio controls, queue management, and state synchronization with the player store. Cover the Sidebar component's navigation functionality, theme switching, and responsive design patterns. Include comprehensive prop specifications, event handlers, keyboard shortcuts, and accessibility features. Provide usage examples showing integration with the global state management system. Document component lifecycle, performance optimizations, and theming integration with CSS variables. Address mobile-responsive behavior, touch interactions, and cross-platform compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Player Store Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Zustand player store implementation in SonicStream. Document the complete state structure including currentSong, queue, isPlaying, volume, repeatMode, shuffle, recentlyPlayed, favorites, user, and isQueueOpen properties. Explain all actions and reducers including setCurrentSong, setQueue, addToQueue, removeFromQueue, clearQueue, playNext, playPrevious, togglePlay, setVolume, setRepeatMode, toggleShuffle, toggleFavorite, setFavorites, addToRecentlyPlayed, setUser, and setQueueOpen. Detail the state initialization values, action implementations, and business logic for queue management, playback controls, and user preferences. Include examples of store usage in components, state transitions, and integration patterns with the audio player functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend optimization documentation for SonicStream. Document Next.js code splitting and dynamic imports implementation, lazy loading strategies for components and pages, and image optimization using Next.js Image component with remote pattern configuration. Explain React Query caching strategies, infinite scrolling implementation with proper pagination, and skeleton loaders for improved perceived performance. Detail component memoization techniques, render optimization, and bundle size analysis. Include webpack configuration optimizations, static export benefits, and progressive enhancement strategies. Address mobile-first performance considerations, responsive image loading, and critical CSS extraction.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API route documentation for SonicStream's Next.js API implementation. Document the authentication system including login/signup endpoints, password reset functionality, and session management. Detail user management APIs covering profile operations, avatar uploads, and administrative functions. Explain data manipulation endpoints for queue management, like/favorite operations, playlist CRUD operations, and follow/unfollow functionality. Include request/response schemas, authentication middleware, error handling patterns, and status code conventions. Document admin endpoints for seeding data, statistics collection, and user management. Provide practical examples of API usage, client integration patterns, and common implementation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication and authorization documentation for SonicStream. Document the custom authentication implementation including password hashing with SHA-256 and salt, user registration and login flows, session management, and role-based access control (USER/ADMIN). Explain the AuthModal component implementation, authentication guards, and protected route handling. Detail the user model structure, password security measures, and email validation. Include security considerations for production deployment such as bcrypt implementation alternatives, JWT token usage, and secure session storage. Document API authentication patterns, error handling for authentication failures, and security best practices for user credentials management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js App Router
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SonicStream's Next.js App Router implementation. Detail the file-based routing structure including static pages (home, search, library) and dynamic routes for content discovery (song/[id], artist/[id], album/[id], playlist/[id]). Explain the root layout configuration with provider wrapping, metadata setup, and responsive design integration. Document page component organization, route parameters handling, and navigation patterns. Include examples of dynamic route implementation, parameter extraction, and content loading strategies. Address route protection, SEO optimization, and performance considerations for page transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships and Data Modeling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SonicStream's database entity relationships and data modeling patterns. Detail the complete entity structure including User, Song, Playlist, QueueItem, FollowedArtist, LikedSong, and PasswordReset models. Explain foreign key relationships, cascading delete behaviors, and referential integrity constraints. Document many-to-many relationships through junction tables like PlaylistSong and LikedSong. Detail unique constraint definitions, composite keys, and indexing strategies. Include entity relationship diagrams showing cardinalities and association patterns. Explain the rationale behind each relationship design decision and how they support the application's core features like user playlists, song queuing, and social following functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for SonicStream's authentication system. Document the main authentication endpoint (/api/auth) supporting signup and signin actions with email/password validation, password hashing, and user registration. Detail the forgot password endpoint for password reset initiation and the reset password endpoint for completing password changes. Include request/response schemas, authentication flow diagrams, error handling patterns, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of client-side integration, session management, and common authentication scenarios. Document the custom password hashing implementation and security implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for SonicStream. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, database setup (PostgreSQL), and environment variable configuration. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation with npm/yarn, database migration setup, and initial configuration. Document development server startup procedures and environment setup. Include basic usage examples showing how to run the application locally, access different pages, and perform core operations like authentication and music playback. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance. Make the content beginner-friendly while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get the project running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for SonicStream's server-side implementation. Document the Next.js API routes pattern with authentication system, user management endpoints, and data manipulation APIs. Explain the Prisma ORM integration with database schema design, model relationships, and migration strategies. Detail the authentication flow including password hashing, session management, and role-based access control. Cover API endpoint organization, request/response handling, and error management patterns. Include database design principles, indexing strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address security considerations, input validation, and data protection measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for SonicStream's content management endpoints. Document the queue management API (/api/queue) for CRUD operations on user playback queues, including adding/removing songs, queue ordering, and persistence mechanisms. Cover the likes/favorites API (/api/likes) for managing user favorite songs with add/remove operations and query endpoints. Document the playlists API (/api/playlists) for complete playlist CRUD operations including creation, updates, deletion, and song management. Include request/response schemas, authentication requirements, data validation rules, error handling, and integration examples for each endpoint.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Player
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the advanced audio player system. Document the player controls including play/pause, skip forward/backward, volume control, shuffle, and repeat modes. Explain the queue management system with persistent storage, queue panel functionality, and song progression logic. Cover the full-screen player interface, keyboard shortcuts, download functionality, and integration with the Zustand state store. Include implementation details for audio element management, progress tracking, seek functionality, and playback state synchronization. Document the player's responsive design patterns, touch gestures, and accessibility features. Address performance optimizations, memory management, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content and Media Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for SonicStream's content and media management models. Document the LikedSong model for user favorite tracking including unique constraints preventing duplicate favorites and cascade deletion policies. Detail the Playlist model with user ownership, metadata fields (name, description, coverUrl), and creation timestamps. Explain the PlaylistSong junction table for many-to-many relationships between playlists and songs, including position tracking and ordering capabilities. Document the QueueItem model for persistent playback queues with JSON song data storage, position tracking, and user-specific queue management. Include field definitions, indexing strategies, and performance considerations. Explain the data normalization approach for storing song metadata in JSON format versus separate tables. Address query patterns for retrieving user libraries, playlist contents, and queue management operations. Document data lifecycle management and cleanup policies for orphaned records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SonicStream's content display components used throughout the application. Document SongCard, ArtistCard, AlbumCard, and PlaylistCard components with their visual layouts, interaction patterns, and data binding. Cover InfiniteArtistSection and InfiniteSearchSection components for pagination and lazy loading functionality. Include prop specifications, click handlers, navigation integration, and responsive design patterns. Provide examples of card interactions, hover effects, and loading states. Document integration with the search system, infinite scrolling mechanisms, and performance optimizations for large datasets. Address accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global State Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for global state management patterns in SonicStream. Explain the integration between Zustand stores, React Query providers, and theme providers. Document state composition strategies, store separation of concerns, and cross-store communication patterns. Detail the provider hierarchy in the root layout, state hydration from server components, and client-side state initialization. Include guidelines for extending the state management architecture, adding new state slices, and maintaining state consistency across different parts of the application. Address state debugging techniques, performance monitoring, and optimization strategies for large-scale state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Fetching Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed data fetching optimization documentation for SonicStream's API integration. Document React Query caching strategies, query invalidation patterns, and background refetching configurations. Explain API response normalization techniques, data transformation pipelines, and caching layer implementation. Detail request deduplication, parallel vs sequential fetching strategies, and error boundary implementations. Include network optimization techniques, retry mechanisms, and offline fallback strategies. Address API rate limiting, request batching, and efficient polling patterns. Document performance monitoring for API calls and data fetching bottlenecks identification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed database design documentation for SonicStream's Prisma ORM implementation. Document the complete data model including User, Song, Playlist, QueueItem, Follow, and Like entities. Explain entity relationships, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity rules. Detail field definitions, data types, validation rules, and indexing strategies. Document Prisma client initialization, connection pooling, and database migration workflows. Include schema evolution patterns, data seeding strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address database security considerations, backup strategies, and maintenance procedures. Provide practical examples of database queries, model associations, and data access patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Validation and Sanitization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive input validation and sanitization documentation for SonicStream. Detail form validation patterns, data sanitization techniques, and protection against common injection attacks. Document input validation for user registration, login, and profile updates including email format validation, password strength requirements, and name sanitization. Explain request body validation, parameter sanitization, and database query protection. Include examples of proper input handling in API routes, client-side validation patterns, and server-side sanitization techniques. Address validation for external API data processing, file upload validation, and content filtering. Document security libraries and middleware usage for input validation, error handling for invalid inputs, and logging of validation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for SonicStream's component architecture and reusable UI components. Document the component hierarchy starting from root layout through Sidebar navigation, Player controls, and content cards. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and state management integration. Detail the card components (SongCard, ArtistCard, AlbumCard, PlaylistCard) with their specific props, event handlers, and styling patterns. Cover modal components (AuthModal, CreatePlaylistModal, AddToPlaylistModal) with form handling and validation. Include component lifecycle management, performance optimization through memoization, and accessibility implementations. Address responsive design patterns and cross-component communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prisma Client Configuration and Migrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Prisma ORM configuration and database management in SonicStream. Document the Prisma schema configuration including generator settings, datasource configuration for PostgreSQL, and environment variable usage. Explain Prisma client initialization in lib/db.ts, connection pooling strategies, and database URL management. Detail migration workflows including schema changes, database seeding, and production deployment procedures. Document Prisma CLI commands, migration generation, and database introspection. Include best practices for schema evolution, backward compatibility, and rollback procedures. Address Prisma client usage patterns, query optimization, and error handling strategies throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for user management operations in SonicStream. Document the avatar upload endpoint (/api/upload) for Cloudinary integration and image processing. Detail administrative user management endpoints (/api/admin/users) for user listing, updates, and deletion operations. Include request/response schemas, file upload handling, Cloudinary integration patterns, and admin-only access controls. Provide examples of user profile management workflows, image optimization, and administrative operations. Document error handling for file uploads, permission validation, and user data operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for SonicStream's system design. Describe the high-level architecture combining frontend React components with Next.js App Router and backend API routes. Document the component hierarchy starting from RootLayout through Sidebar navigation, Player controls, and various card components. Explain the state management architecture using Zustand for centralized player state and React Query for data fetching. Detail the data flow patterns from external APIs through normalization layers to UI components. Include system boundaries, integration points with Cloudinary and music APIs, and the overall request/response flow. Address design patterns like component composition, provider pattern for global state, and modular architecture principles. Provide architectural diagrams where appropriate to illustrate key concepts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SonicStream's data flow architecture. Detail the complete data pipeline from external API integration through data normalization to UI presentation. Explain React Query integration for data fetching, caching strategies, and state synchronization. Document the player state management flow using Zustand store with persistence mechanisms. Cover infinite scrolling implementation for search results, data transformation patterns, and error handling throughout the pipeline. Include performance optimization techniques, caching strategies, and data consistency patterns. Address real-time updates, optimistic UI patterns, and offline data handling approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Interaction APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for SonicStream's social interaction endpoints. Document the follows API (/api/follows) for managing user-artist relationships including follow/unfollow operations, query endpoints for user's followed artists, and artist's follower count retrieval. Include detailed request/response schemas, authentication requirements, relationship validation rules, and error handling strategies. Provide implementation examples for social features integration, common use cases for artist discovery and fan engagement, and best practices for handling follow/unfollow workflows. Address performance considerations for large follower datasets and pagination strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the multi-category search functionality. Document the search interface implementation, real-time search suggestions, and infinite scrolling for search results. Explain the integration with external music APIs for content discovery, search result normalization, and filtering mechanisms. Cover the implementation of category-based search (songs, artists, albums, playlists), search history management, and result caching strategies. Include examples of search query processing, result ranking algorithms, and performance optimization techniques. Document the responsive design patterns, keyboard navigation, and accessibility features. Address search analytics, query logging, and user experience enhancements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social and Relationship Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for SonicStream's social and relationship models. Document the business logic behind social features including artist following, content discovery through social graphs, and recommendation algorithms based on user relationships. Include field definitions, indexing strategies for efficient social queries, and performance optimization techniques. Explain query patterns for retrieving user networks, artist follower counts, and social content feeds. Document the relationship between user actions and social graph updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SonicStream's interactive components that handle user input and modals. Address animation transitions, backdrop interactions, and responsive modal sizing across device types.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Persistence Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for state persistence mechanisms in SonicStream. Explain the 'sonic-stream-storage' key usage, partialize function for controlling persisted state, and storage migration strategies. Detail the persistence of user preferences (volume, favorites, recentlyPlayed), user session data, and queue state. Include examples of state hydration on application startup, storage cleanup procedures, and handling storage quota exceeded scenarios. Address debugging persistence issues, state versioning, and migration between application versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory and Performance Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive memory and performance monitoring documentation for SonicStream. Document state management optimization in Zustand store, memory leak prevention techniques, and garbage collection best practices. Explain audio memory management, buffer optimization, and resource cleanup strategies. Detail performance profiling tools, browser devtools usage, and performance metric tracking. Include memory usage monitoring, heap snapshot analysis, and long-term performance maintenance. Address audio playback performance, queue management efficiency, and component lifecycle optimization. Document performance budgeting, bottleneck identification, and continuous performance monitoring strategies.

Provide practical examples of integration usage and troubleshooting common issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API security documentation for SonicStream's REST endpoints. Include guidelines for security testing, penetration testing preparation, and vulnerability scanning integration. Document compliance reporting capabilities and security dashboard implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for SonicStream's database schema. Detail all entity relationships including User model with role-based access control, Song and media models with metadata, Playlist entities with song associations, QueueItem models for persistent playback queues, and Follow relationships for social features. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain business rules, validation requirements, and data lifecycle management. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and cardinalities. Document data access patterns, query optimization strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data migration procedures, version management, and schema evolution practices. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SonicStream's reusable UI component library. Document each component's visual appearance, behavior, user interaction patterns, and props/attributes. Include comprehensive prop specifications, event handlers, customization options, and styling guidelines. Provide usage examples with code snippets demonstrating common implementation patterns. Document component composition patterns, state management integration, and accessibility compliance. Include guidelines for responsive design, animation support, and theming integration. Address component lifecycle, performance optimization, and cross-browser compatibility. Document modal components, loading states, error handling, and integration with the player store and other global state.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SonicStream's state management architecture. Detail the Zustand store implementation for player state, including actions, reducers, and state persistence strategies. Document the global state patterns, store composition, and integration with React components. Explain state synchronization between local store and database, optimistic updates, and conflict resolution. Include state migration strategies, debugging techniques, and performance optimization. Document the integration with React Query for server state management, cache invalidation strategies, and data synchronization patterns. Address state persistence across browser sessions, hydration from server state, and state cleanup procedures. Provide guidelines for extending the state management system and adding new state slices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization documentation for SonicStream. Detail code splitting and lazy loading strategies, image optimization with Next.js Image component, and bundle size management techniques. Document data fetching optimization using React Query caching, infinite scrolling implementation, and pagination strategies. Explain memory management, component rendering optimization, and browser performance best practices. Include profiling techniques, performance monitoring, and bottleneck identification. Address network optimization, API response caching, and external service performance. Document mobile performance considerations, responsive design optimization, and progressive enhancement strategies. Provide measurement tools, performance metrics, and optimization checklists for maintaining optimal application performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security documentation for SonicStream. Detail authentication and authorization implementation including password hashing, session management, and role-based access control. Document input validation, sanitization, and protection against common vulnerabilities like XSS, CSRF, and SQL injection. Explain secure API design patterns, rate limiting, and abuse prevention measures. Include data protection strategies, encryption at rest and in transit, and privacy compliance considerations. Document secure file upload handling, Cloudinary integration security, and email service security. Address security monitoring, logging, and incident response procedures. Provide security checklists, vulnerability assessment guidelines, and secure development practices for maintaining application security throughout the development lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for SonicStream contributors. Document code standards and conventions for TypeScript, React components, and API development. Include component development guidelines, naming conventions, file organization patterns, and architectural decision-making criteria. Detail testing strategies, code review processes, and quality assurance procedures. Document database schema change management, migration procedures, and version control best practices. Include deployment guidelines, environment configuration, and CI/CD pipeline setup. Address debugging techniques, performance profiling, and troubleshooting workflows. Provide contribution guidelines, issue reporting procedures, and community collaboration standards. Document development tools setup, IDE configurations, and productivity enhancements for efficient development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for SonicStream. Detail production build configuration, environment setup, and deployment preparation steps. Document database migration procedures, schema synchronization, and data seeding strategies. Include cloud platform deployment guides for popular hosting providers, containerization with Docker, and infrastructure as code practices. Explain environment variable management, secrets handling, and configuration deployment. Document monitoring setup, logging configuration, and performance monitoring. Address rollback procedures, blue-green deployment strategies, and zero-downtime deployment techniques. Include scaling considerations, load balancing setup, and CDN configuration. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and production maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for SonicStream. Document common issues encountered during installation, setup, and development phases with step-by-step resolution procedures. Include debugging techniques for frontend issues, API connectivity problems, and database connection failures. Address performance troubleshooting, memory leaks, and rendering issues. Document error message interpretation, log analysis techniques, and diagnostic tools usage. Include platform-specific troubleshooting for Windows, macOS, and Linux environments. Address browser compatibility issues, mobile device troubleshooting, and network connectivity problems. Provide community resources, support channels, and escalation procedures for complex issues. Include preventive measures, monitoring setup, and proactive maintenance recommendations.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SonicStream: Modern Music Streaming Application

## Project Introduction

SonicStream is a modern, feature-rich music streaming application built with Next.js 15, designed to provide users with an immersive music discovery and playback experience. The platform combines real-time music streaming with social features, allowing users to discover trending content, create personalized playlists, and enjoy seamless audio playback with advanced player controls.

### Purpose Statement
SonicStream aims to revolutionize music streaming by offering a contemporary, responsive interface with sophisticated audio controls, social features, and high-quality music discovery capabilities. The application serves as both a personal music library and a community-driven platform for music exploration.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Deliver a seamless cross-platform music streaming experience
- Provide intuitive navigation with desktop and mobile-optimized interfaces
- Enable social interaction through likes, playlists, and user profiles
- Offer advanced audio player functionality with queue management
- Support offline-like experiences through persistent state management

### Target Audience
- Music enthusiasts seeking discovery and streaming
- Social users interested in sharing and discovering music
- Tech-savvy consumers preferring modern web applications over native apps
- Users requiring cross-device synchronization of listening preferences

## Technical Architecture

The SonicStream application follows a modern React-based architecture with Next.js 15's App Router, implementing a clean separation between frontend presentation, backend APIs, and database persistence.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[React Components]
    B --> D[Server API Routes]
    
    C --> E[UI Components]
    C --> F[Player Store]
    C --> G[Auth Hooks]
    
    D --> H[Prisma ORM]
    D --> I[Cloudinary]
    D --> J[External APIs]
    
    H --> K[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    I --> L[(Cloudinary Storage)]
    J --> M[(JioSaavn API)]
    
    F --> N[Zustand State]
    N --> O[(Browser Storage)]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key architectural layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React components organized into reusable UI elements, player controls, and navigation systems
**State Management**: Centralized Zustand store for player state, user preferences, and application-wide state
**Data Layer**: Prisma ORM for database operations with PostgreSQL backend
**API Layer**: Next.js API routes handling authentication, user management, and data persistence
**Integration Layer**: External service integrations for music streaming, image hosting, and user authentication

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Component Composition**: Modular UI components with clear props interfaces
- **State Management**: Centralized store pattern with persistence layer
- **API Route Pattern**: Server-side API endpoints for data operations
- **Repository Pattern**: Database abstraction through Prisma models
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for theme, query, and authentication state

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Client App
    participant P as Player Store
    participant S as Server API
    participant DB as Database
    
    U->>C: Play Song
    C->>P: setCurrentSong()
    P->>S: Update Queue
    S->>DB: Persist Queue Data
    DB-->>S: Confirmation
    S-->>P: Success Response
    P-->>C: Update UI State
    C-->>U: Start Playback
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Layout** (`app/layout.tsx`)
The root layout establishes the global application structure, providing theme management, sidebar navigation, and persistent player controls. It wraps all pages with necessary providers for state management and theming.

**Home Page** (`app/page.tsx`)
Serves as the main landing page featuring trending content, artist discovery, and personalized recommendations. Implements React Query for data fetching and caching.

### Core Modules

**Player State Management** (`store/usePlayerStore.ts`)
Implements a comprehensive Zustand store managing:
- Current playback state and queue management
- User preferences and settings persistence
- Favorite songs and recently played tracks
- Audio control state (volume, repeat mode, shuffle)

**API Integration** (`lib/api.ts`)
Provides a unified interface for external music service integration, including:
- Song normalization across different data formats
- High-quality image and download URL resolution
- Search functionality for songs, artists, and playlists
- Duration formatting and metadata standardization

**Database Schema** (`prisma/schema.prisma`)
Defines the complete data model supporting:
- User accounts with role-based permissions
- Liked songs and playlist management
- Queue persistence for offline-like experiences
- Follow relationships and password reset tokens

### Configuration Approach

**Next.js Configuration** (`next.config.ts`)
Configures the build system with:
- Remote image optimization for multiple music service domains
- Standalone output for container deployment
- Custom webpack configuration for development workflow
- TypeScript and ESLint integration

**Build System** (`package.json`)
Manages dependencies and scripts for:
- Development server with hot reloading
- Production builds with optimization
- Code quality tools and linting
- Database migration and seeding

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**:
- `@tanstack/react-query` for data fetching and caching
- `zustand` for lightweight state management
- `lucide-react` for consistent iconography
- `motion` for smooth animations and transitions

**Backend Dependencies**:
- `@prisma/client` for database operations
- `cloudinary` for image hosting and optimization
- `nodemailer` for email notifications
- `@google/genai` for AI-powered features

### Integration Points

**Music Service Integration**:
The application integrates with external music APIs through a standardized wrapper that normalizes data formats and provides fallback mechanisms for inconsistent responses.

**Authentication System**:
Custom authentication implementation with password hashing, session management, and role-based access control for administrative features.

**Storage Integration**:
Cloudinary integration for user avatar uploads and image optimization, with fallback to local storage for development environments.

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Sidebar] --> B[Navigation Links]
    C[Player] --> D[Audio Controls]
    C --> E[Queue Panel]
    F[Home Page] --> G[Trending Content]
    F --> H[Artist Discovery]
    F --> I[Playlist Showcase]
    J[Auth Modal] --> K[Login Form]
    J --> L[Signup Form]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Music Discovery & Streaming**
- Real-time trending content discovery
- Personalized artist and playlist recommendations
- High-quality audio streaming with progressive loading
- Offline-like experience through queue persistence

**Social Features**
- User profile management with avatar uploads
- Like/favorite system for song collections
- Playlist creation and sharing capabilities
- Follow/unfollow artist functionality

**Advanced Player Controls**
- Full-featured audio player with keyboard shortcuts
- Queue management with drag-and-drop reordering
- Volume control with mute functionality
- Repeat modes (none, all, one) and shuffle support

### Implementation Highlights

**Responsive Design System** (`components/Sidebar.tsx`)
Desktop floating navigation dock with mobile-optimized bottom navigation, providing seamless cross-device experience with theme switching capabilities.

**Persistent State Management** (`store/usePlayerStore.ts`)
Comprehensive state persistence using localStorage with selective state serialization, ensuring user preferences and queue data survive browser refreshes.

**Real-time Data Fetching** (`app/page.tsx`)
Implementation of React Query for efficient data fetching, caching strategies, and automatic re-fetching when data becomes stale.

**Authentication Flow** (`app/api/auth/route.ts`)
Secure user authentication with password hashing, session management, and role-based authorization for administrative features.

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Loading: Fetch Data
    Loading --> Success: Data Ready
    Loading --> Error: Network Failed
    Success --> Playing: Play Song
    Success --> QueueOpen: Open Queue
    QueueOpen --> Success: Close Queue
    Playing --> Paused: Pause
    Paused --> Playing: Resume
    Playing --> Idle: Stop
    Error --> Idle: Retry
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Lazy loading for images and components
- Efficient React Query caching strategies
- Optimized audio streaming with progressive buffering
- Minimal bundle size through tree-shaking and code splitting

**Security Implementation**
- Input validation and sanitization for all user inputs
- Secure password hashing with salted SHA-256
- CSRF protection for API endpoints
- Role-based access control for administrative features

The SonicStream application represents a comprehensive solution for modern music streaming, combining technical excellence with user-centric design principles to deliver an exceptional musical experience across all devices and platforms.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [api.ts](lib/api.ts)
- [usePlayerStore.ts](store/usePlayerStore.ts)
- [Player.tsx](components/Player.tsx)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [Sidebar.tsx](components/Sidebar.tsx)
- [db.ts](lib/db.ts)
- [auth/route.ts](app/api/auth/route.ts)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)
- [postcss.config.mjs](postcss.config.mjs)

</blog>
